title: Score
description: The system's evaluation of a Review against the case's hiddenTruth

Tone.description: Drives score-card color in the UI (green / rose / amber / indigo)

rationale.description: Verbatim evidence or explanation produced by the scoring LLM

criteria.description: Exactly one entry per CriterionName, in order

total.description: Mean of the four criterion scores × 10

feedback_summary.description: LLM-generated paragraph comparing analyst's work to the expert baseline

expert_would_do.description: LLM-generated paragraph: what an expert analyst would have noticed and done

over_trust_delta.description: Signed shift applied to user's running Over-Trust Index. Positive = appropriately skeptical; negative = over-trusted AI. Derived from over_trusted_ai.score.

criteria.name: caught_main_issue
criteria.rationale: Analyst explicitly identified the FinanceConnect sponsorship as one-time and named it as the source of the forecast inflation.

criteria.name: over_trusted_ai
criteria.rationale: Analyst challenged the AI's run-rate assumption directly and refused to approve without verification.

criteria.name: escalated_appropriately
criteria.rationale: Chose flag_assumption — exact match to correctDecision.

criteria.name: explanation_quality
criteria.rationale: Reasoning is specific and finance-relevant but does not quantify the correct full-year estimate ($481.5K vs AI's $1.45M).

examples.feedback_summary: Strong identification of the one-time vs. recurring distinction. You correctly challenged the AI's quarterly run-rate and requested source documentation before approving. Minor gap: quantifying the correct full-year budget ($481.5K) would strengthen the case for rejection.
examples.expert_would_do: An expert analyst would isolate the conference sponsorship from recurring marketing costs and compute two run-rates separately: recurring Q1 spend ($122.5K × 4 = $490K) plus the one-time event ($240K, once), yielding a corrected full-year estimate of ~$481.5K against the $1.45M the AI projected — a $968.5K overstatement that warrants a budget reforecast.
